decision: Rename operational scripts to make their data freshness and artifact scope explicit.
details: Use download_project_map_archive.py, refresh_api_cache.py, build_static_map_single.py, build_static_map_split.py, build_geopackage.py, and refresh_and_build_geopackage.py. No compatibility shims were kept because the repository only has one active user.

decision: Anchor GeoPackage relative output paths to the repository root.
details: The ETL production workspace is a UNC path, and cmd.exe falls back to C:\Windows when it cannot use that UNC path as cwd. Repository-root anchoring keeps default and relative GeoPackage outputs under the script workspace while preserving absolute output paths.

decision: Deploy a staged allowlist mirror to the dedicated production code directory.
details: The deploy tool stages source files from scripts, yesab_map, tests, docs, and location_overrides.csv, then mirrors that staged subset to a destination named yesab_map-toy-maker. Generated outputs, metrics, git metadata, and API cache state are excluded so deployment updates code without overwriting production cache/output state.

decision: Make deploy_to_production.py dry-run by default and require --go for mirroring.
details: Default execution now prints the selected files and warns that destination-only files inside the target directory are removed only when --go is used. This prevents accidental deploys and avoids test recursion from paths that call the deploy command without explicit execution intent.

decision: Let dry-run report dirty-checkout deploy outcomes instead of failing early.
details: When the checkout is dirty, default dry-run now prints the plan plus both outcomes: bare --go is blocked, while --go --allow-dirty would proceed after preflight checks. Real --go deploys still refuse dirty trees unless --allow-dirty is set.

decision: Format dirty dry-run output as explicit with-and-without --allow-dirty scenarios.
details: The dry-run output now labels Scenario without --allow-dirty and Scenario with --allow-dirty so operators can compare blocked bare --go behavior against the allow-dirty execution path without inferring from adjacent status lines.

decision: Harden deploy mirroring and completion reporting after review.
details: Manifest writing now happens only after a successful smoke check. Mirror engines copy staged files first and delete destination-only files only after copy success. CLI deploys now require the default production destination unless --allow-any-dest is passed.

decision: Run the deployed GeoPackage refresh with an explicit uv --directory argument.
details: The FME/cmd environment can start from a UNC current directory that cmd.exe cannot use. The deployed ETL command and smoke check now point uv at the maker directory explicitly and run the script relative to that directory, reducing dependence on the process current directory.

decision: Treat an empty argv list as explicit in refresh_api_cache.main.
details: The GeoPackage wrapper intentionally forwards [] when no cache flags are requested. refresh_api_cache.main now checks argv is None instead of using argv or sys.argv[1:], preventing the wrapper output path from being reparsed during step 2.

decision: Keep the static canvas/grid as the default basemap and make network basemaps opt-in.
details: The map builders now emit a basemap chooser whose default option makes no external requests. Online topographic and imagery choices request viewport-sized ArcGIS MapServer export images in EPSG:3578, preserving the local Yukon Albers canvas projection while allowing users with network access to add context.

decision: Use Government of Yukon cached tile basemaps instead of Esri viewport exports.
details: The opt-in online basemaps now draw Yukon_Basemap_Cache and ShadedRelief_Cache tiles from mapservices.gov.yk.ca in EPSG:3578. This keeps the local default self-contained while replacing slow full-viewport export requests with cached tile row/column requests aligned to the existing Yukon Albers canvas.

decision: Separate AI token cost from human attention cost in the development-cost report.
details: The report values human attention at CAD $100/hr and keeps agent compute as GPT-5.5-normalized token spend. Early pre-metrics work is estimated from commit history and later measured Codex sessions because local non-Codex artifacts did not expose reliable token counters.

decision: Support browser-local KML and SHP/DBF imports without adding runtime dependencies.
details: The static map now loads .kml files by projecting WGS84 lon/lat into Yukon Albers in the browser and loads .shp geometry with optional same-stem .dbf attributes. ZIP/KMZ and arbitrary shapefile projection reprojection are left out to preserve the self-contained no-library output model; local SHP coordinates are assumed to already match the map projection.

decision: Auto-project geographic local shapefile coordinates into Yukon Albers.
details: Local SHP imports now detect degree-like lon/lat record bounds and project points with the same in-browser Yukon Albers math used by KML. This fixes datasets such as D:\\data\\Hex_4.shp whose PRJ is geographic NAD83 CSRS and previously counted features but drew them near degree coordinates instead of the map coordinate space.
